# terrible_purestorage_flashblade_purefb_pingtrace (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `destination` (String) IP addtress or hostname used to run ping or trace against.
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `action` (String) Which action is required, ping or trace
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `component` (String) Used by ping and trace to specify where to run the operation. Valid values are controllers and blades from hardware list. If not specified defaults to all available controllers and selected blades
- `count` (Number) Used by ping to specify the number of packets to send
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `discover_mtu` (Boolean) Used by trace to specify whether or not to discover the MTU along the path being traced
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `fragment` (Boolean) Used by trace to specify whether or not to fragment packets
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `latency` (Boolean) Specify whether or not to print the full user-to-user latency
- `method` (String) Used by trace to specify the method to use for operations
- `packet_size` (Number) Used by ping to specify the number of data bytes to send per packet
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `port` (String) Used by trace to specify a destination port
- `resolve` (Boolean) Specify whether or not to map IP addresses to host names
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `source` (String) IP address or hostname used by ping and trace to specify where to start to run the specified operation If not specified will use all available sources
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
